description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a pitched wooden roof supported by two vertical wooden posts. The stone base is constructed from stacked, gray stones, forming a sturdy cylindrical wall. The roof is made of reddish-brown wooden shingles arranged in a triangular gable style, providing shelter over the well opening. A wooden crossbeam is visible beneath the roof, likely serving as a support for a bucket or pulley mechanism, though no bucket or rope is clearly visible. The well sits on a patch of green grass, suggesting it is placed outdoors, and the area around the base is slightly worn. The overall design is rustic and functional, typical of wells found in parks, gardens, or village squares.
description: A rustic stone water well with a pitched wooden shingle roof and sturdy stone base, set on a grassy patch. The well features wooden supports and a crossbeam beneath the roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open well mouth, wooden crossbeam, and pitched roof with visible support posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the solid stone base and the full length of the wooden shingle roof, with no visible opening.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the well mouth, crossbeam, and roof structure from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the stone base and the roof's side profile.
attribute_well_cover_design: No cover; the well mouth is open beneath a pitched wooden roof.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: None; only a crossbeam is visible, with no bucket or pulley present.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a low circular stone wall and covered by a wooden roof supported by posts.
